item.title: Pakistan forces hit military facilities in Afghanistan
item.description: Pakistan said on Sunday its forces had attacked military facilities in southern Afghanistan, as well as ‘terrorist hideouts’, in the latest strikes between the two sides....

item.title: BNP lawmaker questions equating Liberation War with 2024 uprising
item.description: The member of parliament for the Cumilla-6 constituency, Md Monirul Haque Chowdhury, on Saturday criticised what he described as a growing tendency to equate the 1971 War of Independence with the 2024 mass uprising, saying that living......

item.title: Lailatul Qadr tonight
item.description: The holy Shab-e-Qadr, also known as Lailatul Qadr, when the first verses of the holy Quran were revealed to Prophet Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH), will be observed across the country Monday night....

item.title: BMU outpatient department to remain open on March 19, 22
item.description: During the Eid-ul-Fitr holidays, the outpatient department of Bangladesh Medical University will be open on March 19 and March 22 for the convenience of patients....News analysis reports

item.title: Rajuk officials among 6 sued over approval of building plan violating rules
item.description: The Anti-Corruption Commission on Sunday filed a case against six people, including five former and serving officials of the Rajdhani Unnayan Kartripakkha, over allegations of approving a 10-storey building plan in violation of conditions in the capital’s Uttarkhan area....

item.title: Court freezes Flight Expert’s 20 bank accounts
item.description: A Dhaka court has ordered the freezing of 20 bank accounts belonging to online ticket booking company FEBD Limited, Flight Expert, and its chairman Salman Bin Rashid Shah Sayem....

item.title: Govt releases 5 prisoners on Eid-ul-Fitr
item.description: On the occasion of Eid-ul-Fitr, the government has released five convicted prisoners, who had served more than half of their sentences from various jails across the country....

item.title: Coast Guard arrests 7 ‘robbers’ with arms in Khulna
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard arrested seven members of a robber group named Khabir Molla along with weapons and ammunition at Dighalia in Khulna early Sunday....Bangladesh news updates

item.title: Heads face MPO suspension for not replying to broadsheet
item.description: Education ministry said that the Monthly Pay Order of educational institutions’ heads would be suspended or cancelled if they failed to send broadsheet replies of inspection and audit reports within the stipulated time....

item.title: Diesel shortage halts Rajshahi waste removal
item.description: Residents of Rajshahi City Corporation on Sunday continued to suffer immensely, as an acute crisis of diesel left waste removal operations in the city disrupted, leaving piles of garbage along roads and in neighbourhoods....

item.title: DMP advises city residents to keep valuables with relatives, police
item.description: The Dhaka Metropolitan Police on Sunday advised city residents to ensure safekeeping of valuables at relatives’ homes, or respective police stations if needed, before embarking on their Eid holiday journey. ...Breaking news alerts

item.title: PM to launch canal excavation project in 54 districts today
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman is set to inaugurate a nationwide canal excavation and re-excavation programme in 54 districts out of 64 today....

item.title: 500 ME flights cancelled in 16 days
item.description: At least 500 Middle Eastern flights were cancelled to and from Hazrat Shahjalal International Airport in Dhaka city in the past 16 days since February 28 till Sunday when 23 flights were cancelled....

item.title: Khondkar Delwar’s 15th death anniv today
item.description: The 15th anniversary of death of Language Movement hero, freedom fighter and former secretary general of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party, Khondkar Delwar Hossain, will be observed today....

item.title: Caretaker govt system to take effect after 13th parliament: SC
item.description: The Appellate Division of the Supreme Court has ruled that the non-party caretaker government system will come into effect only after the 13th parliament completes its tenure or is dissolved earlier....News website membership

item.title: Flight disruptions worry stuck migrant workers
item.description: Several thousand Bangladeshi migrant workers bound for Gulf countries are passing their days in uncertainty after flights have been suspended following the Middle East conflict....

item.title: BNP leaders appointed administrators of 42 zila parishads
item.description: The Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government on Sunday appointed BNP leaders as new administrators of 42 zila parishads across the country, pursuing its move to place new administrators at local government bodies....

item.title: Poll campaign in Bogura-6, Sherpur-3 begins today
item.description: Election campaign in the parliamentary election in Sherpur-3 and by-election in Bogura-6 will begin on Monday as election commission allotted election symbols among the candidates......

item.title: Ministry asks progress reports on campaign activities at edn instts
item.description: The education ministry on Sunday instructed officials concerned to send progress report of activities at all educational institutions under it to prevent drug addiction, sexual harassment and disorder in the name of Rag Day by March 30......Maps

item.title: Europe and the war on Iran
item.description: EUROPEAN nations are joining the United States and Israel in their war on Iran. From providing economic and diplomatic support, to supplying military assistance to Washington, Europe is moving from a passive supporter to an active participant in the campaign to overthrow the Iranian government......

item.title: Safeguarding children
item.description: INTERNATIONAL Women’s Day was, no doubt, celebrated with visible fervour as a new government is in office, but was that not somewhat disingenuous given the horrific and distressing incidents of child gang rape and murder weighing heavily on the public conscience? Added to them are the disturbing facts that the police were reluctant to do their job in the immediate......

item.title: Bangladesh’s food security journey
item.description: BANGLADESH’S journey towards food security is a story of resilience, innovation and ongoing challenges. From the days of famine of 1974 (March to December) and food aid (Red Cross aid and soup kitchens) to becoming largely self-sufficient in staple crops, the country has made remarkable progress. Yet, as the population grows and climate change intensifies......Bangladesh news updates

item.title: River’s quiet warning
item.description: A VISIT to the River Jamuna near Sirajganj offers a striking and unsettling picture of the fragile river landscape. Beneath the bridge, the river appeared unusually shallow, its vast bed exposed and layered with sand. Yet, along other stretches of the riverbank, water crept dangerously close to human settlements. Houses that once stood at a safe distance now face the......

item.title: Continuous vigilance must for safe Eid journey
item.description: THE government announcement that strict action will be taken against unfit vehicles and other traffic irregularities during the Eid-time travel is both timely and welcome. Every year, millions of people leave the capital to celebrate the festival with their families, creating immense pressure on roads, terminals and transport services. In such circumstances, the move to stop......

item.title: Govt should make labour migration fair, humane
item.description: THE overseas labour migration sector has almost always been mired in problems. The sector now appears not to be able to withstand the pressure of its own growth. Official documents show that the number of recruiting agencies has almost tripled, from 923 in 2015 to 2,677 now, the highest among the countries that send migrant workers overseas. The sector has......

item.title: Content creator Rakib killed by unidentified killers at Shaheed Minar
item.description: A social media content creator was gunned down by unidentified killers in the capital’s Central Shaheed Minar area on Sunday night......Breaking news alerts
